scenario: Team has Web Modeler project. Wants to share with external consultant for review (read-only). Wonders sharing options.
question: Web Modeler sharing / collaboration?

options.text: Web Modeler does not support project sharing or collaboration â€” users must export and share files manually â€” this approach has limitations. Web Modeler in Camunda 8 SaaS supports project-level collaboration with role-based access control. Projects can be shared with other organization members and external collaborators with specific roles: Viewer, Commenter, Editor, or Admin. Project sharing enables team-based BPMN modeling workflows where multiple modelers can work on the same project with appropriate access levels. Real-time collaboration and commenting capabilities support review and approval workflows within the Web Modeler interface.

Web Modeler provides exactly four project sharing roles: Project Admin (edit + invite), Editor (edit, no invite), Commenter (view + comment only, no edit), and Viewer (read-only, no comments). This granularity allows precise access assignment: external reviewers get Commenter role; read-only stakeholders get Viewer; active contributors get Editor; project managers get Project Admin. An all-or-nothing model would prevent the common use case of external stakeholders reviewing diagrams without being able to modify them.

md:27-32 (SaaS only per :12 badge): Project Admin: edit project + all folders/diagrams + invite collaborators. Editor: edit all folders and diagrams (cannot invite). Commenter: cannot edit; can view diagrams + properties + leave comments. ⚠️ Often-missed role -- answers listing only 3 roles (Admin / Editor / Viewer) are incomplete. Viewer: read-only; cannot leave comments either. Super-user mode per :36-49: temporarily grants Project Admin org-wide to assign collaborators to orphaned projects; requires Organization Owner / Admin (SaaS) or Web Modeler Admin role (Self-Managed) per :57-67. External users must be invited to the Camunda 8 organization first (SaaS) or provisioned in Identity / Keycloak (Self-Managed). Best fit for "read + comment external review": Commenter role (NOT Viewer -- Viewer cannot comment).

Web Modeler supports real-time collaborative access by inviting external users to the project with appropriate roles. Invited users can access the live diagram directly in their browser without needing exports. The Commenter role is specifically designed for external reviewers: they can view the diagram and leave contextual comments without edit access. Email-based exports are a workaround that loses context and prevents interactive collaboration.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler sharing: Project-level sharing (per collaboration.md:27-32 -- doc-verbatim 4 roles): Project Admin: edit project + all folders/diagrams + invite collaborators. Editor: edit folders/diagrams (no invite). Commenter: view + leave comments (cannot edit). Viewer: read-only (no comments). Adding members: Project settings → members. Invite users by email / username. Assign role. External users: Must be in Camunda Identity: SaaS: invited to organisation. Self-Managed: created in Keycloak. Auth via SSO (per Set 14 Q54) if configured. For consultants: Add as project member: with View permission. Time-limit access: revoke when engagement ends. Audit: who accessed what. File-level sharing (verify per version): Granular: share specific files, not whole project. Permission per file. For "send for review": Option 1: Share in Web Modeler: Consultant logs in; sees project; reviews. Pros: live model; can comment. Cons: requires Camunda account. Option 2: Export + send file: Download BPMN file. Email to consultant. Pros: no account needed. Cons: static; no comments in tool. Option 3: Public sharing link (verify support): Some tools support read-only shareable links. Verify Web Modeler version. Collaboration features: Comments / annotations: In-tool comments: discussion on specific elements. Threaded: replies. Resolved markers. Version history (per Set 13 Q57): Track changes: who modified what. Diff view. Revert. Real-time collaboration: Multi-user edit: simultaneous (with conflict resolution). Live cursors: see other editors. For external review: Workflow: 1. Invite consultant as project View member. 2. Consultant logs in; views models. 3. Adds comments / annotations. 4. Team responds; iterates. 5. Remove consultant when done. For multi-stakeholder review: Internal team + business stakeholders + external consultants. Each with appropriate permission. Comments enable async discussion. For SaaS: Console manages organisation members. Web Modeler projects within organisation. External: invite to organisation. For Self-Managed: Identity / Keycloak: user management. Project membership: in Web Modeler. Security considerations: Limit external access: time-limited, scope-limited. Audit access: who saw what. Sensitive models: extra care for confidential workflows. Compliance: external access policies. Best practices: Minimum privilege: View for reviewers, Edit only when needed. Time-limit: remove access when done. Document collaborators: track who has access. Use comments: in-tool discussion better than email threads. Anti-patterns: Owner permission for reviewers: too much access. Indefinite external access: forgotten members. Shared accounts: lose audit. For the scenario (external consultant, "read-only review"): If consultant should only view → assign Viewer. If consultant should view + leave comments / annotations (typical for review engagements) → assign Commenter -- this is the right least-privilege choice for a review workflow (Viewer cannot comment per collaboration.md:32). Add consultant to Camunda 8 organization first (SaaS) / Identity (Self-Managed). Remove access (or downgrade) when engagement ends.
